description: Causal world model - tracks cause-effect relationships, counterfactuals, and confidence scores

counterfactuals.prediction: Memory would contain entries from external scans, API key exposure, and destructive operations
counterfactuals.evidence: Safety rules block external targets, credentials, and destructive commands

counterfactuals.prediction: Failures would accumulate without detection, goal drift would go unnoticed, and system health would degrade
counterfactuals.evidence: Daemon runs regular health checks and catches failures early

counterfactuals.prediction: Transient failures would be treated as permanent, reducing overall success rate
counterfactuals.evidence: Retry logic in scheduling loop recovers from transient failures

counterfactuals.prediction: MCR would not learn from past runs, would repeat mistakes, and cannot promote skills
counterfactuals.evidence: memory.jsonl enables pattern detection and skill candidate generation

counterfactuals.prediction: Memory would be polluted with scan results from external systems, breaking safety boundaries
counterfactuals.evidence: Test-SwarmSafety explicitly blocks external targets; memory audit confirms no pollution
